assertions.assertion: The service must be scaffolded as a Hono application with x402-hono middleware in the services/x402-optimizer/ directory structure.

do: Create services/x402-optimizer/src/index.ts as the main Hono app entry point
Use x402-hono paymentMiddleware for payment-gated routes
Structure routes in services/x402-optimizer/src/routes/ (analyze.ts, status.ts)
Place integration logic in services/x402-optimizer/src/lib/ (dispatch.ts, ponder.ts)
Include package.json with hono and x402-hono dependencies
Create .gitignore excluding node_modules/, dist/, .env, *.log
dont: Use Express, Fastify, or other frameworks instead of Hono
Place service files in project root or scattered locations
Skip the x402 payment middleware for the analyze endpoint
Forget to create .gitignore (causes Gemini CLI token overflow from scanning node_modules)

assertions.commentary: Hono + x402-hono is the specified stack. Consistent directory structure makes the service maintainable and follows x402 ecosystem conventions. CRITICAL: Always create .gitignore first to prevent node_modules from being scanned.

assertions.assertion: POST /analyze must accept { endpointUrl: string } and return { jobId: string, statusUrl: string } after dispatching an analysis job.

do: Validate endpointUrl is a valid URL before dispatching
Return 400 with error message for invalid input
Return jobId (the marketplace request ID) and statusUrl pointing to GET /status/:jobId
Protect this endpoint with x402 payment middleware ($0.50 per analysis)
dont: Allow unpaid requests to dispatch jobs

assertions.commentary: The analyze endpoint is the core paid service. It gates job dispatch behind x402 payment.

assertions.assertion: GET /status/:jobId must query Ponder for job status and return { status: 'pending' | 'in_progress' | 'completed' | 'failed', reportUrl?: string }.

do: Query Ponder GraphQL for request status using the jobId as request ID
Map Ponder delivered status to 'completed'
Include reportUrl (IPFS gateway link to deliveryIpfsHash) when status is 'completed'
Return 404 if jobId not found in Ponder
dont: Return stale or cached status without querying Ponder
Expose internal error details to clients

assertions.commentary: Status polling is free (no payment required) and provides visibility into job progress.

assertions.assertion: GET /health must return 200 OK with { status: 'healthy' } for Railway health checks.

do: Return 200 status code with JSON body
Respond quickly without external dependencies
Include timestamp or version in response for debugging
dont: Fail health check due to Ponder connectivity issues
Add payment middleware to health endpoint

assertions.commentary: Health endpoint is required for Railway deployment and should be lightweight.

assertions.assertion: Job dispatch must use the existing marketplaceInteract pattern from the monorepo to post analysis requests to the OLAS mech marketplace.

do: Import and use marketplaceInteract from mech-client-ts
Include the analysis blueprint in IPFS metadata
Pass endpointUrl in additionalContext for the analysis agent
Use environment variables for RPC_URL, MECH_ADDRESS, PRIVATE_KEY

assertions.commentary: Reusing existing dispatch infrastructure ensures consistency and leverages battle-tested code.

assertions.assertion: Ponder queries must use the graphQLRequest helper from http/client.ts to fetch job status with retry logic.

do: Query requests table by id for delivered status and deliveryIpfsHash
Use PONDER_GRAPHQL_URL environment variable for endpoint
Handle GraphQL errors gracefully with appropriate HTTP status codes
dont: Skip retry logic for transient failures
Query Ponder on every incoming request without caching consideration

assertions.commentary: Consistent use of shared HTTP client ensures proper error handling and logging.

assertions.assertion: The /analyze endpoint must be protected by x402-hono paymentMiddleware requiring $0.50 USDC payment on base-sepolia network.

do: Configure paymentMiddleware with PAYMENT_WALLET_ADDRESS from env
Set price to '$0.50' for testnet (can adjust for mainnet)
Use 'base-sepolia' network for development, 'base' for production
Return proper 402 Payment Required response with payment details
dont: Use non-USDC tokens without explicit configuration

assertions.commentary: x402 payment is the core monetization mechanism. Proper middleware setup is essential.

assertions.assertion: The service must include railway.json configuration for Railway deployment with proper build and start commands.

do: Include railway.json with NIXPACKS builder
Set startCommand to 'yarn start' or 'node dist/index.js'
Configure healthcheckPath to '/health'
Document required environment variables in .env.example

assertions.commentary: Railway deployment config ensures one-click deployment capability.

assertions.assertion: The service must include an embedded analysis blueprint that instructs the agent to discover x402 service capabilities, research the ecosystem, and produce optimization recommendations.

do: Include blueprint JSON in services/x402-optimizer/src/lib/analysis-blueprint.ts
Assertions: discover service from URL alone, research 10+ x402 services, compare against 3+ similar services
Assertions: perform live endpoint testing, include 3+ actionable recommendations
Enable web_fetch, google_web_search, create_artifact tools for analysis agent
dont: Require OpenAPI spec or detailed documentation from target service

assertions.commentary: The analysis blueprint defines WHAT the agent must discover and report, making the optimization service valuable.

assertions.assertion: All configuration must be via environment variables with sensible defaults and clear documentation.

do: Document all required env vars: PAYMENT_WALLET_ADDRESS, RPC_URL, MECH_ADDRESS, PRIVATE_KEY, PONDER_GRAPHQL_URL
Provide .env.example with placeholder values
Use base-sepolia as default network for development
Default PORT to 3000
dont: Hardcode any addresses, keys, or URLs

assertions.commentary: Clear environment configuration enables easy local development and production deployment.

assertions.assertion: All API endpoints must be tested by making actual HTTP requests and verifying responses match the specification.

do: Test GET /health returns 200 with status: healthy
Test POST /analyze returns 402 without payment header
Test GET /status/:jobId returns appropriate status structure
Document test commands or scripts for verification
dont: Ship without any verification of endpoint behavior

assertions.commentary: Verification ensures the service actually works as specified before deployment.
